text.title: RSpec Testing Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real RSpec suites.

antipattern.symptom: Suite takes 8 minutes; 90% of specs hit DB via create(:resource).
antipattern.root-cause: Spec authors default to create instead of build_stubbed.
antipattern.fix: Audit specs: where DB persistence not verified, switch to build_stubbed. Suite drops to 1-2 min.

antipattern.symptom: Service specs require Rails boot; CI cold-start = 30s per worker; suite distribution suffers.
antipattern.root-cause: All specs require rails_helper, even pure Ruby tests.
antipattern.fix: Pure-logic specs `require 'spec_helper'`; only request/feature/system specs need rails_helper.

antipattern.symptom: Spec is a flat list of 30 `it` blocks; reviewer can't tell which variant each tests.
antipattern.fix: Group by state: `context 'when order is paid' { it 'returns paid status'; ... }`; failure messages now read 'when order is paid it returns paid status'.

antipattern.symptom: Every spec creates 5 records via let!; specs that don't use them still pay the setup cost.
antipattern.fix: Audit each let!; downgrade to let (lazy) unless the side effect is strictly required.
